spec.title: TCP Errors
spec.description: Tracks various TCP error and congestion-related events, including retransmissions, timeouts, dropped connections, and buffer issues

spec.title: UDP Errors
spec.description: Rate of UDP and UDPLite datagram delivery errors, including missing listeners, buffer overflows, and protocol-specific issues

spec.title: ICMP In / Out
spec.description: Number of ICMP messages sent and received per second, including error and control messages

spec.title: Sockstat UDP
spec.description: Number of UDP and UDPLite sockets currently in use

spec.title: Sockstat FRAG / RAW
spec.description: Number of FRAG and RAW sockets currently in use. RAW sockets are used for custom protocols or tools like ping; FRAG sockets are used internally for IP packet defragmentation

spec.title: Sockstat Used
spec.description: Total number of sockets currently in use across all protocols (TCP, UDP, UNIX, etc.), as reported by /proc/net/sockstat

spec.title: Disk I/O Utilization
spec.description: Percentage of time the disk was actively processing I/O operations

spec.title: Memory DirectMap
spec.description: How much memory is directly mapped in the kernel using different page sizes (4K, 2M, 1G). Helps monitor large page utilization in the direct map region

spec.title: Memory Anonymous
spec.description: Memory used by anonymous pages (not backed by files), including standard and huge page allocations. Includes heap, stack, and memory-mapped anonymous regions

spec.title: Memory Writeback and Dirty
spec.description: Memory currently dirty (modified but not yet written to disk), being actively written back, or held by writeback buffers. High dirty or writeback memory may indicate disk I/O pressure or delayed flushing

spec.legendFormat: Writeback – Memory currently being flushed to disk
Dirty – Memory marked dirty (pending write to disk)
NFS Unstable – Pages sent to NFS server, awaiting storage commit

spec.title: Memory Slab
spec.description: Kernel slab memory usage, separated into reclaimable and non-reclaimable categories. Reclaimable memory can be freed under memory pressure (e.g., caches), while unreclaimable memory is locked by the kernel for core functions

spec.legendFormat: SReclaimable – Potentially reclaimable slab memory (e.g., inode cache)

spec.title: Disk R/W Merged
spec.description: Number of read and write requests merged per second that were queued to the device

spec.title: Memory Committed
spec.description: Displays committed memory usage versus the system's commit limit. Exceeding the limit is allowed under Linux overcommit policies but may increase OOM risks under high load

spec.legendFormat: Committed_AS – Memory promised to processes (not necessarily used)

spec.title: Memory LRU Active / Inactive (%)
spec.description: Proportion of memory pages in the kernel's active and inactive LRU lists relative to total RAM. Active pages have been recently used, while inactive pages are less recently accessed but still resident in memory

spec.legendFormat: Inactive – Less recently used memory, more likely to be reclaimed
Active – Recently used memory, retained unless under pressure

spec.title: Memory Unevictable and MLocked
spec.description: Memory that is locked in RAM and cannot be swapped out. Includes both kernel-unevictable memory and user-level memory locked with mlock()

spec.title: Memory Shared and Mapped
spec.description: Memory used for mapped files (such as libraries) and shared memory (shmem and tmpfs), including variants backed by huge pages

spec.legendFormat: Mapped – Memory mapped from files (e.g., libraries, mmap)
Shmem – Shared memory used by processes and tmpfs
ShmemHugePages – Shared memory (shmem/tmpfs) allocated with HugePages
PMD Mapped – Shmem/tmpfs backed by Transparent HugePages (PMD)

spec.title: Memory HugePages
spec.description: Displays HugePages memory usage in bytes, including allocated, free, reserved, and surplus memory. All values are calculated based on the number of huge pages multiplied by their configured size

spec.title: Network Traffic Compressed
spec.description: Rate of compressed network packets received and transmitted per interface. These are common in low-bandwidth or special interfaces like PPP or SLIP

spec.title: Network Traffic Errors
spec.description: Rate of packet-level errors for each network interface. Receive errors may indicate physical or driver issues; transmit errors may reflect collisions or hardware faults

spec.title: Network Traffic Drop
spec.description: Rate of dropped packets per network interface. Receive drops can indicate buffer overflow or driver issues; transmit drops may result from outbound congestion or queuing limits

spec.title: Network Traffic Fifo
spec.description: Tracks FIFO buffer overrun errors on network interfaces. These occur when incoming or outgoing packets are dropped due to queue or buffer overflows, often indicating congestion or hardware limits

spec.title: Network Traffic Frame
spec.description: Rate of frame errors on received packets, typically caused by physical layer issues such as bad cables, duplex mismatches, or hardware problems

spec.title: Network Traffic Multicast
spec.description: Rate of incoming multicast packets received per network interface. Multicast is used by protocols such as mDNS, SSDP, and some streaming or cluster services

spec.title: Processes Forks
spec.description: Rate of new processes being created on the system (forks/sec).

spec.title: Exporter Processes Memory
spec.description: Tracks the memory usage of the process exposing this metric (e.g., node_exporter), including current virtual memory and maximum virtual memory limit

spec.title: Entropy
spec.description: Number of bits of entropy currently available to the system's random number generators (e.g., /dev/random). Low values may indicate that random number generation could block or degrade performance of cryptographic operations

spec.title: Disk Space Used Basic
spec.description: Percentage of filesystem space used for each mounted device

spec.expr: ((node_filesystem_size_bytes{instance="$node", job="$job", device!~'rootfs'} - node_filesystem_avail_bytes{instance="$node", job="$job", device!~'rootfs'}) / node_filesystem_size_bytes{instance="$node", job="$job", device!~'rootfs'}) * 100

spec.title: Root FS Used
spec.description: Used Root FS

spec.title: Sys Load
spec.description: System load over all CPU cores together

spec.title: Filesystem Used
spec.description: Disk usage (used = total - available) per mountpoint

spec.title: Node Exporter Scrape
spec.description: Shows whether each Node Exporter collector scraped successfully (1 = success, 0 = failure), and whether the textfile collector returned an error.

spec.title: Hardware Temperature Monitor
spec.description: Monitors hardware sensor temperatures and critical thresholds as exposed by Linux hwmon. Includes CPU, GPU, and motherboard sensors where available

spec.title: RAM Used
spec.description: Real RAM usage excluding cache and reclaimable memory

spec.title: Memory Kernel / CPU / IO
spec.description: Tracks kernel memory used for CPU-local structures, per-thread stacks, and bounce buffers used for I/O on DMA-limited devices. These areas are typically small but critical for low-level operations

spec.legendFormat: PerCPU – Dynamically allocated per-CPU memory (used by kernel modules)
Bounce Memory – I/O buffer for DMA-limited devices

spec.title: Time Synchronized Status
spec.description: Shows whether the system clock is synchronized to a reliable time source, and the current frequency correction ratio applied by the kernel to maintain synchronization

spec.title: Memory Page Faults
spec.description: Rate of memory page faults, split into total, major (disk-backed), and derived minor (non-disk) faults. High major fault rates may indicate memory pressure or insufficient RAM

spec.legendFormat: Pgfault - Page major and minor fault ops

matcher.options: Pgfault - Page major and minor fault ops

spec.title: Memory Pages In / Out
spec.description: Rate of memory pages being read from or written to disk (page-in and page-out operations). High page-out may indicate memory pressure or swapping activity

spec.title: Memory LRU Active / Inactive Detail
spec.description: Breakdown of memory pages in the kernel's active and inactive LRU lists, separated by anonymous (heap, tmpfs) and file-backed (caches, mmap) pages.

spec.legendFormat: Inactive_file - File-backed memory on inactive LRU list
Inactive_anon – Anonymous memory on inactive LRU (incl. tmpfs & swap cache)
Active_file - File-backed memory on active LRU list
Active_anon – Anonymous memory on active LRU (incl. tmpfs & swap cache)

spec.title: CPU Busy
spec.description: Overall CPU busy percentage (averaged across all cores)

spec.title: SWAP Used
spec.description: Percentage of swap space currently used by the system

spec.title: File Nodes Size
spec.description: Number of file nodes (inodes) available per mounted filesystem. Reflects maximum file capacity regardless of disk size

spec.title: Memory Pages Swap In / Out
spec.description: Rate at which memory pages are being swapped in from or out to disk. High swap-out activity may indicate memory pressure

spec.title: Sockstat Memory Size
spec.description: Kernel memory used by TCP, UDP, and IP fragmentation buffers

spec.title: Netstat IP In / Out Octets
spec.description: Rate of octets sent and received at the IP layer, as reported by /proc/net/netstat

spec.title: Disk IOps
spec.description: Disk I/O operations per second for each device

spec.title: ARP Entries
spec.description: Number of ARP entries per interface. Useful for detecting excessive ARP traffic or table growth due to scanning or misconfiguration

spec.title: Network Traffic Carrier Errors
spec.description: Rate of carrier errors during transmission. These typically indicate physical layer issues like faulty cabling or duplex mismatches

spec.title: Network Traffic Collision
spec.description: Rate of packet collisions detected during transmission. Mostly relevant on half-duplex or legacy Ethernet networks

spec.title: Memory
spec.description: Breakdown of physical memory and swap usage. Hardware-detected memory errors are also displayed

spec.legendFormat: Apps - Memory used by user-space applications
PageTables - Memory used to map between virtual and physical memory addresses
SwapCache - Memory that keeps track of pages that have been fetched from swap but not yet been modified
Slab - Memory used by the kernel to cache data structures for its own use (caches like inode, dentry, etc)
Cache - Parked file data (file content) cache
Hardware Corrupted - Amount of RAM that the kernel identified as corrupted / not working

matcher.options: Hardware Corrupted - Amount of RAM that the kernel identified as corrupted / not working

spec.title: IRQ Detail
spec.description: Breaks down hardware interrupts by type and device. Useful for diagnosing IRQ load on network, disk, or CPU interfaces. Requires --collector.interrupts to be enabled in node_exporter

spec.title: Time Synchronized Drift
spec.description: Tracks the system clock's estimated and maximum error, as well as its offset from the reference clock (e.g., via NTP). Useful for detecting synchronization drift

spec.title: File Descriptor
spec.description: Number of file descriptors currently allocated system-wide versus the system limit. Important for detecting descriptor exhaustion risks

spec.title: Speed
spec.description: Maximum speed of each network interface as reported by the operating system. This is a static hardware capability, not current throughput

spec.title: MTU
spec.description: MTU (Maximum Transmission Unit) in bytes for each network interface. Affects packet size and transmission efficiency

spec.title: Softnet Packets
spec.description: Packets processed and dropped by the softnet network stack per CPU. Drops may indicate CPU saturation or network driver limitations

spec.title: Time PLL Adjust
spec.description: NTP phase-locked loop (PLL) time constant used by the kernel to control time adjustments. Lower values mean faster correction but less stability

spec.title: Systemd Sockets Accepted
spec.description: Rate of accepted connections per second for each systemd socket

spec.title: Systemd Units State
spec.description: Current number of systemd units in each operational state, such as active, failed, inactive, or transitioning

spec.title: TCP In / Out
spec.description: Rate of TCP segments sent and received per second, including data and control segments

spec.title: CPU
spec.description: CPU time usage split by state, normalized across all CPU cores

spec.legendFormat: System - Processes executing in kernel mode
User - Normal processes executing in user mode
Nice - Niced processes executing in user mode
Iowait - Waiting for I/O to complete
Steal - Time spent in other operating systems when running in a virtualized environment
Idle - Waiting for something to happen

spec.expr: sum by(instance) (rate(node_cpu_guest_seconds_total{instance="$node",job="$job"}[$__rate_interval])) / on(instance) group_left sum by (instance)((rate(node_cpu_seconds_total{instance="$node",job="$job"}[$__rate_interval]))) > 0

matcher.options: Idle - Waiting for something to happen
Iowait - Waiting for I/O to complete
Nice - Niced processes executing in user mode
Steal - Time spent in other operating systems when running in a virtualized environment
System - Processes executing in kernel mode
User - Normal processes executing in user mode

spec.title: Cooling Device Utilization
spec.description: Shows how hard each cooling device (fan/throttle) is working relative to its maximum capacity

spec.title: Disk Ops Discards / Flush
spec.description: Per-second rate of discard (TRIM) and flush (write cache) operations. Useful for monitoring low-level disk activity on SSDs and advanced storage

spec.title: Power Supply
spec.description: Shows the online status of power supplies (e.g., AC, battery). A value of 1-Yes indicates the power supply is active/online

spec.title: CPU Saturation per Core
spec.description: Shows CPU saturation per core, calculated as the proportion of time spent waiting to run relative to total time demanded (running + waiting).

spec.title: CPU Schedule Timeslices
spec.description: Rate of scheduling timeslices executed per CPU. Reflects how frequently the scheduler switches tasks on each core

spec.title: OOM Killer
spec.description: Rate of Out-of-Memory (OOM) kill events. A non-zero value indicates the kernel has terminated one or more processes due to memory exhaustion

spec.title: Exporter Process CPU Usage
spec.description: Rate of CPU time used by the process exposing this metric (user + system mode)

spec.title: Network Operational Status
spec.description: Operational and physical link status of each network interface. Values are Yes for 'up' or link present, and No for 'down' or no carrier.

spec.title: Softnet Out of Quota
spec.description: How often the kernel was unable to process all packets in the softnet queue before time ran out. Frequent squeezes may indicate CPU contention or driver inefficiency

spec.title: PIDs Number and Limit
spec.description: Number of active PIDs on the system and the configured maximum allowed. Useful for detecting PID exhaustion risk. Requires --collector.processes in node_exporter

spec.title: Threads Number and Limit
spec.description: Number of active threads on the system and the configured thread limit. Useful for monitoring thread pressure. Requires --collector.processes in node_exporter

spec.title: Processes Detailed States
spec.description: Current number of processes in each state (e.g., running, sleeping, zombie). Requires --collector.processes to be enabled in node_exporter

spec.title: TCP Stat Persistent
spec.description: Number of TCP sockets in key connection states. Requires the --collector.tcpstat flag on node_exporter

spec.title: CPU Frequency Scaling
spec.description: Real-time CPU frequency scaling per core, including average minimum and maximum allowed scaling frequencies

spec.title: Pressure Stall Information
spec.description:
How often tasks experience CPU, memory, or I/O delays. 'Some' indicates partial slowdown; 'Full' indicates all tasks are stalled. Based on Linux PSI metrics:
https://docs.kernel.org/accounting/psi.html

spec.title: Pressure
spec.description: Resource pressure via PSI

spec.title: Hardware Fan Speed
spec.description: Displays the current fan speeds (RPM) from hardware sensors via the hwmon interface

spec.title: Disk Sectors Discarded Successfully
spec.description: Shows how many disk sectors are discarded (TRIMed) per second. Useful for monitoring SSD behavior and storage efficiency

spec.title: Network Traffic NoHandler
spec.description: Rate of received packets that could not be processed due to missing protocol or handler in the kernel. May indicate unsupported traffic or misconfiguration

spec.title: Disk Read/Write Data
spec.description: Number of bytes read from or written to the device per second

spec.title: Softnet RPS
spec.description: Tracks the number of packets processed or dropped by Receive Packet Steering (RPS), a mechanism to distribute packet processing across CPUs

spec.title: Systemd Sockets Current
spec.description: Current number of active connections per systemd socket, as reported by the Node Exporter systemd collector

spec.title: Systemd Sockets Refused
spec.description: Rate of systemd socket connection refusals per second, typically due to service unavailability or backlog overflow

spec.title: PPS Frequency / Stability
spec.description: Displays the PPS signal's frequency offset and stability (jitter) in hertz. Useful for monitoring high-precision time sources like GPS or atomic clocks

spec.title: PPS Time Accuracy
spec.description: Tracks PPS signal timing jitter and shift compared to system clock

spec.title: PPS Sync Events
spec.description: Rate of PPS synchronization diagnostics including calibration events, jitter violations, errors, and frequency stability exceedances

spec.title: TCP/UDP Kernel Buffer Memory Pages
spec.description: TCP/UDP socket memory usage in kernel (in pages)

spec.title: UDP Queue
spec.description: Number of UDP packets currently queued in the receive (RX) and transmit (TX) buffers. A growing queue may indicate a bottleneck

spec.title: Network Saturation
spec.description: Network interface utilization as a percentage of its maximum capacity

spec.title: Sockstat Average Socket Memory
spec.description: Average memory used per socket (TCP/UDP). Helps tune net.ipv4.tcp_rmem / tcp_wmem

spec.title: Instantaneous Queue Size
spec.description: Number of in-progress I/O requests at the time of sampling (active requests in the disk queue)

spec.title: TCP Socket Queue
spec.description: TCP socket queue sizes. High rx_queued_bytes indicates application not reading fast enough. High tx_queued_bytes indicates network congestion or slow receiver

spec.title: TCP Stat Transient
spec.description: Transient TCP connection states. These are typically short-lived during connection establishment and teardown

spec.title: Average Queue Size
spec.description: Average queue length of the requests that were issued to the device

spec.title: Time Spent Doing I/Os
spec.description: Percentage of time the disk spent actively processing I/O operations, including general I/O, discards (TRIM), and write cache flushes

spec.title: Disk Average Wait Time
spec.description: Average time for requests issued to the device to be served. This includes the time spent by the requests in queue and the time spent servicing them.

spec.title: Node Exporter Scrape Time
spec.description: Duration of each individual collector executed during a Node Exporter scrape. Useful for identifying slow or failing collectors

spec.title: File Nodes Free
spec.description: Number of free file nodes (inodes) available per mounted filesystem. A low count may prevent file creation even if disk space is available

spec.title: Disk Throughput
spec.description: Disk I/O throughput per device

spec.title: Filesystem Space Available
spec.description: Amount of available disk space per mounted filesystem, excluding rootfs. Based on block availability to non-root users

spec.title: Filesystem in ReadOnly / Error
spec.description: Indicates filesystems mounted in read-only mode or reporting device-level I/O errors.

spec.title: ICMP Errors
spec.description: Rate of incoming ICMP messages that contained protocol-specific errors, such as bad checksums or invalid lengths

spec.title: UDP In / Out
spec.description: Rate of UDP datagrams sent and received per second, based on /proc/net/netstat

spec.title: Network Traffic by Packets
spec.description: Number of network packets received and transmitted per second, by interface.

spec.title: NF Conntrack
spec.description: Current and maximum connection tracking entries used by Netfilter (nf_conntrack). High usage approaching the limit may cause packet drops or connection issues

spec.title: Processes Status
spec.description: Processes currently in runnable or blocked states. Helps identify CPU contention or I/O wait bottlenecks.

spec.title: Sockstat TCP
spec.description: Tracks TCP socket usage and memory per node

spec.title: Exporter File Descriptor Usage
spec.description: Number of file descriptors used by the exporter process versus its configured limit

spec.title: System Load
spec.description: System load average over 1, 5, and 15 minutes. Reflects the number of active or waiting processes. Values above CPU core count may indicate overload

spec.title: Memory Vmalloc
spec.description: Usage of the kernel's vmalloc area, which provides virtual memory allocations for kernel modules and drivers. Includes total, used, and largest free block sizes

spec.legendFormat: Vmalloc Free Chunk – Largest available block in vmalloc area
Vmalloc Total – Total size of the vmalloc memory area
Vmalloc Used – Portion of vmalloc area currently in use

spec.title: Network Traffic Basic
spec.description: Per-interface network traffic (receive and transmit) in bits per second

spec.title: CPU Basic
spec.description: CPU time spent busy vs idle, split by activity type

spec.title: Memory Basic
spec.description: RAM and swap usage overview, including caches

spec.title: Context Switches / Interrupts
spec.description: Per-second rate of context switches and hardware interrupts. High values may indicate intense CPU or I/O activity

spec.title: TCP Direct Transition
spec.description: Rate of TCP connection initiations per second. 'Active' opens are initiated by this host. 'Passive' opens are accepted from incoming connections

spec.title: Network Traffic
spec.description: Incoming and outgoing network traffic per interface

spec.title: TCP Connections
spec.description: Number of currently established TCP connections and the system's max supported limit. On Linux, MaxConn may return -1 to indicate a dynamic/unlimited configuration

spec.title: Disk Read/Write IOps
spec.description: Number of I/O operations completed per second for the device (after merges), including both reads and writes

spec.title: TCP SynCookie
spec.description: Rate of TCP SYN cookies sent, validated, and failed. These are used to protect against SYN flood attacks and manage TCP handshake resources under load
